engineering.title: General Engineering
engineering.overview: Engineering is the practical application of science and mathematics to solve real-world problems. A general engineering degree provides flexibility, allowing you to explore multiple disciplines in your first year before specialising in mechanical, civil, electrical, chemical or aeronautical engineering from year 2. UK engineering is globally renowned, with the highest concentration of engineering research investment of any country. Graduates enter careers across infrastructure, manufacturing, energy, technology and aerospace sectors.
skills: Mathematical modelling and computational analysis (MATLAB, CAD software)
Technical writing and communication to non-specialists
Health and safety compliance (ISO standards, COSHH)

professionalBodies.name: Engineering Council (EC)
professionalBodies.benefit: BEng from an accredited programme is the first stage towards Chartered Engineer (CEng) status. MEng meets the academic requirements for CEng.

professionalBodies.name: Institution of Mechanical Engineers (IMechE)
professionalBodies.benefit: Specialised accreditation for mechanical engineering degrees; supports pathway to Chartered Mechanical Engineer (CMechE).

professionalBodies.name: Institution of Civil Engineers (ICE)
professionalBodies.benefit: Specialised accreditation for civil engineering; leads to Chartered Civil Engineer (CEng).

engineering.employerPerspective: Engineering graduates are among the most versatile technical professionals in the job market. Multinational engineering firms (Arup, Atkins, Jacobs) run dedicated graduate schemes targeting MEng graduates specifically. The construction and infrastructure sectors have acute skills shortages. Technology and aerospace firms recruit heavily from engineering programmes for both technical and graduate management roles.
engineering.entryRequirements: Typical offer: A*AA–AAA at A-level depending on university. Mathematics is essential at virtually all institutions; Physics is required or strongly recommended. Further Maths is advantageous. GCSE Maths at grade 7/A or above is commonly required.
engineering.furtherStudy: MEng Integrated Masters, Postgraduate Diplomas in Specialisation (e.g., Structural Engineering, Environmental Engineering), MBA or Management Masters, PhD in Engineering, Chartered Engineer (CEng) accreditation pathway.
isItForYou: Study this if you want to solve practical problems at scale — building infrastructure, designing machines, improving energy systems.
Study this if you enjoy both theory and hands-on work — UK engineering degrees balance rigorous maths with laboratory and design projects.
Study this if you value global job security — engineering is a portable, internationally recognised qualification; graduates work across the world.
Think twice if you dislike mathematics — strength in pure and applied mathematics is essential throughout; differential equations and numerical analysis are core.
Think twice if you want to avoid long hours — final-year projects and design work can be intensive; professional practice expects high standards.

mechanical.title: Mechanical Engineering
mechanical.overview: Mechanical engineering is the broadest engineering discipline, bridging physics and practical design to create machines, engines, systems and processes. From thermodynamics and fluid mechanics to structural analysis and control systems, mechanical engineers are involved in everything from Formula 1 vehicles to offshore wind turbines to medical devices. The UK has world-leading mechanical engineering research and strong links to automotive, aerospace and energy sectors.

modules: Specialist Modules (Hybrid Vehicles, Renewable Energy, etc.)

skills: Finite Element Analysis (FEA) and Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD)
Written and oral communication of technical concepts

professionalBodies.name: Institution of Mechanical Engineers (IMechE)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited MEng leads to Chartered Mechanical Engineer (CMechE) status with further experience. Access to professional networks, journals and CPD.

mechanical.employerPerspective: Mechanical engineering graduates are highly sought after by automotive (JLR, Bentley), aerospace (Rolls-Royce, BAE) and energy companies. The sector has chronic skills shortages in thermodynamics and fluid dynamics expertise. Graduate schemes at major companies are highly competitive and favour MEng graduates with project experience.
mechanical.entryRequirements: Typical offer: A*AA–AAA. Maths and Physics A-levels essential. Further Maths strongly preferred. GCSE Maths at grade 7 or above required.
mechanical.furtherStudy: MEng Integrated Masters, MSc in Automotive/Aerospace/Energy Engineering, PhD in Mechanical Engineering, MBA, Professional Engineer accreditation.
isItForYou: Study this if you want to design and optimise machines, engines and systems.
Study this if you enjoy thermodynamics and fluid dynamics — these are core and you'll use them throughout your career.
Study this if you want maximum career options — mechanical skills transfer across automotive, aerospace, energy and manufacturing.
Think twice if you find mathematics abstract — you'll need strong calculus and differential equations applied to real systems.
Think twice if you prefer pure theory over design — this degree emphasises practical application and iterative design.

civil.title: Civil Engineering
civil.overview: Civil engineering is the oldest engineering discipline, creating and maintaining the physical infrastructure that society depends on: buildings, bridges, roads, water systems, and public infrastructure. UK civil engineers work on iconic projects globally — from HS2 in the UK to the Channel Tunnel to major infrastructure across Europe and the Middle East. The sector offers extraordinary diversity: structural design, geotechnical engineering, water engineering, transportation, and sustainable infrastructure.
skills: Structural analysis and design (finite element analysis, CAD software like AutoCAD/Revit)

professionalBodies.name: Institution of Civil Engineers (ICE)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited MEng leads to Chartered Civil Engineer (CEng) status. Strong professional networks, CPD requirements, and access to projects and innovations.

civil.employerPerspective: Major infrastructure projects (HS2, Thames Tunnel, major road/rail upgrades) create continuous demand for civil engineers. Arup and Atkins run world-class graduate schemes. The sector faces significant skills gaps in geotechnical and structural engineering. Large construction companies actively recruit MEng graduates into graduate management roles.
civil.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAA–AAB. Maths and Physics essential. Further Maths useful. GCSE Maths at grade 7/A or above required.
civil.furtherStudy: MEng, MSc in Structural / Geotechnical / Water Engineering, PhD, MBA, Chartered Engineer pathway.
isItForYou: Study this if you want to contribute to large-scale infrastructure projects that millions of people use.
Study this if you enjoy working across multiple disciplines — structures, geology, hydrology, and project management all feature.
Study this if you value stability — infrastructure will always be needed; civil engineers are always in demand.
Think twice if you prefer pure research over applied work — civil engineering is about solving real-world problems under budget and time constraints.
Think twice if you want to avoid fieldwork — site visits and ground investigation are core parts of the job.

electrical.title: Electrical Engineering
electrical.overview: Electrical engineering underpins modern life, from power systems supplying electricity to homes, to electronics in smartphones, to renewable energy systems and autonomous vehicles. UK electrical engineering is at the forefront of innovation in power systems, renewable energy integration, electric vehicles and advanced electronics. Graduates work in energy, telecommunications, aerospace, medical devices and consumer electronics.

modules: Power Systems and Smart Grids (or Electronics Specialisation)
High Voltage Engineering or Advanced Microelectronics

skills: FPGA and microcontroller programming (VHDL, Verilog, C/C++)
Power systems analysis and load flow
Signal processing and control systems design

professionalBodies.name: Institution of Engineering and Technology (IET)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited MEng leads to Chartered Engineer (CEng) status. Strong community, professional development and networking in electrical and electronic engineering.

electrical.employerPerspective: Electrical engineers are among the most sought-after graduates. Smart grid modernisation and renewable energy integration create massive demand for power systems specialists. Embedded systems expertise is highly valued in automotive (EV transition) and consumer electronics. Graduate schemes at utilities, defence contractors and tech firms are highly competitive.
electrical.entryRequirements: Typical offer: A*AA–AAB. Maths essential; Physics strongly recommended. Further Maths advantageous. GCSE Maths at grade 7 or above required.
electrical.furtherStudy: MEng, MSc in Power Systems / Electronics / Energy Engineering, PhD, MBA, Chartered Engineer pathway.
isItForYou: Study this if you're fascinated by electricity, electronics and how devices work.
Study this if you want to contribute to the energy transition — renewable energy integration is the defining challenge of your career.
Study this if you enjoy both theory and hands-on lab work — electrical engineering requires rigorous analysis and practical verification.
Think twice if you don't enjoy advanced mathematics — electromagnetism and control theory are abstract and mathematically demanding.
Think twice if you prefer macro-scale projects over detailed design — much electrical engineering involves careful circuit design and signal integrity.
